rounds.prompt: How many times farther is the nearest star than the Sun?
rounds.fact: The Sun is ~150 million km away; Proxima Centauri is ~40 trillion km — 267,000× further. Light takes 4.2 years to get there.

rounds.prompt: How many times wider is the observable universe than the Milky Way?
rounds.fact: The Milky Way is ~100,000 light-years across; the observable universe is ~93 billion light-years — 930,000× wider.

rounds.prompt: How many times farther is Neptune from the Sun than the Earth is?
rounds.fact: Earth is 1 AU from the Sun; Neptune is ~30 AU — 30× farther. Sunlight takes 8 minutes to reach Earth, and over 4 hours to reach Neptune.

rounds.prompt: How many times wider is the Sun than the Moon?
rounds.fact: The Moon is 3,475 km wide; the Sun is 1,392,700 km — 400× wider. This is also exactly how much closer the Moon is, making total eclipses possible.

rounds.prompt: How many times more massive is the Sun than Earth?
rounds.fact: Earth weighs ~6 × 10²⁴ kg; the Sun weighs ~2 × 10³⁰ kg — 333,000× more massive. The Sun contains 99.86% of all mass in the solar system.
